title: eriogonum microtheca
intro: Eriogonum microtheca, also known as the San Diego buckwheat or desert buckwheat, is a flowering plant that belongs to the buckwheat family (Polygonaceae). The plant has been traditionally used in Native American medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits associated with Eriogonum microtheca include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and support immune function. Eriogonum microtheca can be prepared into herbal remedies, such as teas, infusions, and salves, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
